name: Fatty Acid Beta-Oxidation
description: Fatty acid beta-oxidation: activated fatty acyl-CoA is shuttled into the mitochondrion by carnitine, then each cycle of oxidation, hydration, oxidation and thiolytic cleavage shortens the chain by two carbons, yielding acetyl-CoA, FADH2 and NADH.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Fatty acid activated (acyl-CoA)"]
    N2["Carnitine shuttle (CPT1)"]
    N3["Acyl-CoA DH: FADH2"]
    N4["Enoyl-CoA hydratase"]
    N5["3-hydroxyacyl-CoA DH: NADH"]
    N6["Thiolase: cleave acetyl-CoA"]
    N7["Chain shortened by 2C"]
    N8{"Carbons remain?"}
    N9["Acetyl-CoA -> Krebs/ketones"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N6 --> N9
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|yes: next cycle| N3
    N8 -->|no| N9

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
